items.boardQuestion: Where do we already have realized savings proof that leadership can reuse with confidence?
items.currentPosture: Procurement packaging is consolidated and cycle-time evidence is available, but the board still needs one cleaner proof packet.
items.requiredProof: Cycle-time drop, duplicate wrapper retirements, and one surviving answer core.
items.headline: Procurement already has the cleanest realized-savings proof in the estate.
items.narrative: This lane can anchor the efficiency story because duplicate work is retired, cycle-time evidence exists, and ownership stayed intact.
items.nextMove: Package procurement cycle-time proof into one board-ready packet with wrapper-retirement evidence attached.

items.efficiencyTheme: Protect control quality while tightening execution proof
items.boardQuestion: Which lane should stay funded but still needs tighter owner follow-through proof?
items.currentPosture: Identity remains defensible, but remediation follow-through and cost-avoidance proof are not yet packaged cleanly for the board.
items.requiredProof: Remediation closure timing, avoided exposure narrative, and ownership continuity.
items.headline: Identity needs tighter follow-through proof more than it needs a new savings target.
items.narrative: The lane is strategically protected, but the operating story still needs stronger proof that owners closed the loop on efficiency and risk reduction.
items.nextMove: Tighten remediation closure evidence and package cost-avoidance proof before the next board packet.

items.efficiencyTheme: Demonstrate that trimmed backlog turned into margin recovery
items.boardQuestion: Where can we prove that efficiency moves produced cleaner margin instead of just fewer projects?
items.currentPosture: Low-yield backlog is down, but leadership still needs one tighter proof room that shows what was cut and what margin stayed protected.
items.requiredProof: Backlog reduction, margin recovery continuity, and reporting simplification evidence.
items.headline: Revenue can now prove that efficiency cuts protected the margin engine instead of weakening it.
items.narrative: The CFO-safe story is no longer just about removing work; it is about showing the margin core stayed intact while noise and backlog fell.
items.nextMove: Publish one margin-recovery packet that connects backlog reduction to protected yield and cleaner reporting.

items.efficiencyTheme: Escalate thin proof before making another board claim
items.boardQuestion: Which lane still has too much leakage or too little proof to support a confident board story?
items.currentPosture: Payments, treasury, merchant review, and KYC are live, but proof still fragments across fragile surfaces and ownership gaps.
items.requiredProof: Unified proof on payout risk, queue health, and review closure across the FinTech stack.
items.headline: FinTech still needs escalation because the efficiency story is not board-safe yet.
items.narrative: The right move is to escalate proof gaps and leakage instead of overclaiming realized efficiency that is still too fragile to defend.
items.nextMove: Escalate FinTech proof gaps into one diligence packet before any new savings or investment claim reaches leadership.

items.efficiencyTheme: Sustain the regulated stack and prove nothing critical slipped
items.boardQuestion: Which regulated lane should be sustained because the efficiency story depends on zero control erosion?
items.currentPosture: The quality and safety stack behaves like one mature operating layer, but leadership still wants proof that efficiency pressure did not degrade controls.
items.requiredProof: Continuity proof across GxP, CAPA, specimen, safety, and narrative review.
items.headline: Biotech should sustain its current operating layer and prove control quality held through every efficiency move.
items.narrative: The board story here is not bigger savings; it is that the regulated layer stayed intact while the estate got leaner around it.
items.nextMove: Sustain the biotech stack and publish continuity proof that every regulated control held through the last efficiency cycle.

items.efficiencyTheme: Prove flagship leverage after satellite consolidation
items.boardQuestion: Where can we show that consolidation produced operating leverage instead of just another AI narrative?
items.currentPosture: AI satellites are collapsed into the flagship governance lane, but leadership still needs one concise proof room that shows leverage landed.
items.requiredProof: Satellite retirements, flagship reuse, and board-memo reuse across the AI stack.
items.headline: AI can now prove operating leverage if flagship reuse and satellite retirements are shown together.
items.narrative: The board story gets stronger when AI shows fewer wrappers, more flagship reuse, and one clearer decision spine instead of scattered wins.
items.nextMove: Package flagship reuse, memo reuse, and retired satellite evidence into one operating-leverage packet.
